engineCodes.h2: Chevrolet Engine Codes & Average Rebuilt Prices (UK Supply)
engineCodes.h3: Compare costs for every major Chevrolet engine — VCDi diesel family (Z-series), B10/B12 small petrols, and LS V8 performance engines. All prices are typical UK market ranges for professionally rebuilt engines with warranty. Click "Get Quote" on any engine to receive tailored prices from our network of UK Chevrolet specialists.

groups.name: VCDi Diesel Family
groups.failureNote: The VM Motori-derived VCDi turbo-diesel family powered the majority of Chevrolet's UK fleet across Captiva, Cruze, Orlando, and Malibu. Known for injector sensitivity and timing chain stretch on later variants . Get Quote → for any VCDi engine above. Common failure points: VCDi engines are known for fuel injector sensitivity to UK diesel quality and timing chain stretch on Z22D1/Z20D1 variants. Our rebuilt units include reconditioned injectors, genuine timing kits, and upgraded chain tensioners.

groups.name: Small Petrol Family
groups.failureNote: These economical 1.0–1.3 litre petrol and diesel engines powered Chevrolet Spark, Aveo, and Kalos models. Simple, robust units with affordable rebuild costs . Get Quote → for any Small Petrol engine above. Common failure points: B-series engines are generally reliable but suffer coil pack failures and thermostat housing leaks. Our rebuilt units include new ignition components and upgraded cooling system seals.

groups.name: LS V8 Performance Family
groups.failureNote: The legendary GM LS V8 — found in imported Camaro, Corvette, and Silverado models in the UK. These engines are often retrofitted into other vehicles, reflected in the higher rebuild costs and specialist parts . Get Quote → for any LS V8 engine above. Common failure points: LS engines are renowned for reliability, but high-mileage units suffer lifter tick, oil pump wear, and cracked exhaust manifolds. Our rebuilt LS engines receive upgraded lifters, high-volume oil pumps, and performance gaskets.

groups.name: Additional Petrol Engines
groups.failureNote: Petrol options for Chevrolet's SUV and saloon range, often overlooked but still searched for replacement. Get Quote → for any Additional Petrol engine above. Common failure points: GM petrol 2.4L engines suffer timing chain stretch and EGR issues. The 3.0/3.2L V6 units have known camshaft actuator problems. Our rebuilds include OEM timing chains and redesigned actuators

commonProblems.h2: Common Chevrolet Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Chevrolet’s UK presence is defined by two very different engine families: the VCDi diesel range (2.0 and 2.2 litres, co-developed with VM Motori, fitted to Captiva and Cruze) and the small-capacity petrol engines (1.0/1.2 litres, found in Spark and Aveo). VCDi units dominate replacement queries due to timing chain stretching, fuel system failures, and crankshaft bearing issues — often making replacement better value than a full rebuild. Every rebuilt Chevrolet engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the most common failures, what genuine repair options cost at a dealer vs a specialist, and when to walk away from a repair entirely.

problems.h4: VCDi Timing Chain Failure – The Diesel Engine’s Most Common Weak Point
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Captiva 2.0 VCDi (2006–2015, Z20S, Z20S1, Z20D1), Chevrolet Cruze 2.0 VCDi (2009–2015, Z20D1), Chevrolet Orlando 2.0 VCDi (2011–2015, Z20D1)
problems.rootCause: Overly long timing chain design combined with inadequate oil flow to the chain tensioner. Oil service intervals that stretch to 20,000 miles accelerate chain wear, leading to slack, rattling on cold start, and eventual jumping of sprockets.
problems.recommendation: On a Chevrolet Captiva worth £2,000–£4,000, a dealer timing chain replacement is rarely economical. Specialist preventative replacement (£900–£1,300) is the best value if caught early — before the rattle becomes constant. Once the chain has jumped and bent valves, skip the major repair and go straight to a reconditioned VCDi engine with upgraded timing components. The total cost is often only £1,000 more than a cylinder head rebuild, and you get a full warranty.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt Z20D1 / Z20S engine prices for your Captiva or Cruze

problems.group: VCDi Fuel Injector & High-Pressure Pump Failure
problems.h4: VCDi Fuel Injector & High-Pressure Pump Failure – The Expensive Diesel Problem
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Captiva 2.0 VCDi & 2.2 VCDi (2006–2015, Z20S1, Z22D1), Chevrolet Cruze 2.0 VCDi (2009–2015, Z20D1)
problems.rootCause: The Delphi common-rail fuel system on VCDi engines suffers from high-pressure pump internal wear, which sends metallic debris through the injectors, fuel rail, and return lines. Once the pump fails, the entire fuel system is contaminated, and flushing rarely succeeds long-term.
problems.recommendation: Do not throw money at single injector replacements once pump failure has been confirmed. The debris is already everywhere. For a vehicle worth under £3,000, a full fuel system overhaul at a specialist (£1,600–£2,400) is acceptable. For higher-value or higher-mileage vehicles, we strongly recommend a reconditioned VCDi engine with a new fuel system already fitted — it removes all contamination risk and costs less than a dealer fuel system repair.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for Z22D1 reconditioned engines with new Delphi fuel systems

problems.group: Small Petrol Engine Cylinder Head Cracking
problems.h4: Small Petrol Engine Cylinder Head Cracking – Spark & Aveo Failure
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Spark 1.0 (2005–2015, B10D1, B10S1), Chevrolet Spark 1.2 (2005–2015, B12D1), Chevrolet Aveo 1.2 (2006–2015, B12D1)
problems.rootCause: The cast aluminium cylinder head on these small petrol engines develops microscopic cracks between valve seats, typically due to thermal cycling and inadequate cooling system maintenance. Coolant loss, external weeps, and eventually combustion gas ingress into cooling system follow. The head is not reliably repairable.
problems.recommendation: Do not attempt to repair a cracked head. The success rate is low, and the labour cost to revisit is painful. On a Spark or Aveo worth £1,000–£2,000, a used engine with verified mileage (£1,600–£2,200 supplied & fitted) is the most economical route. For owners planning to keep the car, a reconditioned long engine with a warranty is worthwhile — the price difference versus a specialist replacement head is often only £400–£600.
problems.cta: → Compare B10D1 and B12D1 used & reconditioned engine prices for your Spark or Aveo

problems.h4: VCDi Crankshaft Bearing & Bottom-End Failure – The Sudden Seizure Risk
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Captiva 2.0 VCDi (2006–2011, Z20S, Z20S1), Chevrolet Cruze 2.0 VCDi (2009–2012, Z20D1)
problems.rootCause: Oil pump drive gear wears prematurely on early VCDi engines, reducing oil flow at idle and low RPM. This starves the main and big-end bearings, leading to spun bearings, knocking, low oil pressure warning, and eventually complete seizure. There is no warning period — failure is rapid.
problems.recommendation: If your VCDi engine has spun a bearing or seized, do not attempt an in-chassis crank grind. By the time bearing failure occurs, the crankshaft is usually damaged beyond reliable regrinding, and the block mating surface may be compromised. A reconditioned VCDi engine with an uprated oil pump drive is the industry-standard fix. Specialist supply & fit pricing (£2,800–£4,200) makes this a predictable, warrantied solution.
problems.cta: → Get instant quotes for rebuilt Z20S engines with uprated oil pumps

problems.h4: VCDi Turbocharger Failure – Boost Leaks, Overboost & Oil Starvation
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Captiva 2.0 VCDi & 2.2 VCDi (2006–2015, Z20S1, Z22D1), Chevrolet Cruze 2.0 VCDi (2009–2015, Z20D1)
problems.rootCause: Variable geometry turbo vanes stick due to carbon build-up from short journeys and low-quality oil. Simultaneously, the turbo oil feed pipe restricts over time, reducing oil supply to the CHRA (bearing housing). The result: overboost codes, smoke, loss of power, and eventually a seized turbo sending debris into the intercooler.
problems.recommendation: On a higher-mileage VCDi engine, replacing just the turbo without addressing potential oil debris in the sump and cooler carries risk. Specialist turbo replacement with full system flush (£1,300–£1,900) is the minimum reliable repair. If the engine already has over 120,000 miles and suffered a catastrophic turbo failure, price up a reconditioned engine with a new turbo — the additional £1,500–£2,500 buys a complete fresh engine with warranty instead of a patch-up on a tired bottom end.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt Z22D1 engines with new turbochargers for Captiva 2.2 VCDi

problems.group: Petrol Engine Camshaft Position Sensor & Timing Issues
problems.h4: Petrol Engine Camshaft Position Sensor & Timing Issues – 1.4 & 1.6 Family
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Cruze 1.4 Turbo (2009–2015, B14NET, A14NET), Chevrolet Cruze 1.6 (2009–2015, B16D1), Chevrolet Orlando 1.8 (2011–2015, F18D4)
problems.rootCause: The camshaft position sensors fail intermittently due to poor heat shielding, causing rough running, stalling, and ‘limp home’ mode. On B14NET engines, the variable valve timing (VVT) sprockets can also wear, causing timing correlation codes. Left unaddressed, cam timing drift can lead to valve-to-piston contact on interference designs.
problems.recommendation: If you get intermittent camshaft correlation codes, replace both cam sensors at a specialist (£120–£200) before assuming the worst. This solves the majority of early failures. However, if the issue persists after new sensors and a confirmed good oil supply, budget for VVT sprockets and a timing chain. On a Cruze 1.4 Turbo worth £2,000–£3,000, a full used engine (£2,000–£3,000 supplied & fitted) is an acceptable alternative to a complex VVT repair at a main dealer.
problems.cta: → Compare B14NET reconditioned engine prices for your Cruze 1.4 Turbo

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Chevrolet
finalCta.paragraph: The failures above — timing chains, fuel system contamination, bearing seizure, and cracked heads — account for the majority of Chevrolet engine replacement enquiries in the UK. In almost every case, a reconditioned or low-mileage used engine from a specialist supplier offers better value than a dealer-level major repair, particularly on vehicles over eight years old. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Chevrolet specialists, with every rebuilt engine including upgraded components designed to address the original weak points. You also get a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, nationwide delivery, and the choice of supply-only or supply & fit. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Chevrolet Engine Replacement Prices Now
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Chevrolet model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Chevrolet Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Chevrolet engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle's age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Chevrolet Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the factory price. For VCDi diesel units, this includes reconditioned injectors and new timing components.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,500 – £3,800 (depending on engine code)

types.title: Chevrolet Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as uprated timing chain tensioners for VCDi engines, reinforced piston rings for A12XER petrols, and high‑volume oil pumps for LS V8s – for a longer service life than a standard replacement.

types.title: Used Chevrolet Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available. Popular for Captiva and Spark models where used units are readily available from UK breakers.

types.title: Chevrolet Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for performance models (Camaro/Corvette LS engines) or owners wanting a zero‑mile engine with maximum peace of mind.

types.title: Chevrolet Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn't had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older or budget‑sensitive models (Aveo, Kalos, early Spark) where a full recon isn't economical.

types.title: Chevrolet Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Chevrolet specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting of the replacement (including new gaskets, fluids, and filters), and a workmanship warranty. Choose supply-only or supply & fit depending on whether you already have a trusted garage.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £400 – £800 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Chevrolet engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Chevrolet model today.

engineSizes.h2: Chevrolet Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Chevrolet engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Chevrolet 1.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.7-litre diesel was fitted primarily to the Chevrolet Cruze saloon and hatchback (2011–2015) and represented Chevrolet’s attempt at a fuel-efficient, lower-CO2 entry point for fleet and private buyers. This size uses a single engine code, the 1.7 VCDi, which is an Isuzu-derived unit. Replacement demand for this size is lower than the 2.0-litre VCDi family, but failures do occur — typically around the DPF (diesel particulate filter) and EGR system, which become blocked on short-journey cars
items.cta: Get quotes for Chevrolet 1.7L diesel engines

items.title: Chevrolet 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre VCDi diesel is Chevrolet’s most common and most replacement-demand diesel engine in the UK, fitted across three core models: Captiva, Cruze, and Orlando. Developed with VM Motori, the 2.0 VCDi exists in multiple power outputs and uses several engine codes (Z20S, Z20S1, Z20D1). These engines dominate replacement enquiries due to timing chain stretch, which often jumps and bends valves, and high-pressure fuel pump failure that contaminates the entire injection system. A reconditioned 2.0 VCDi with upgraded timing components is the market-standard fix
items.cta: Get quotes for Chevrolet 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Chevrolet 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2-litre VCDi diesel is a larger-displacement evolution of the 2.0-litre engine, fitted exclusively to the later Chevrolet Captiva (2011–2015). It shares the same architecture as the 2.0-litre but uses a longer stroke to increase torque, making it better suited for the heavy Captiva SUV. Engine code Z22D1 identifies this 2.2 VCDi unit. Replacement demand is driven by the same failures as the 2.0-litre family: timing chain issues that require full engine replacement, and fuel system contamination from high-pressure pump debris. Reconditioned 2.2 VCDi engines routinely include uprated timing chains and new Delphi fuel systems
items.cta: Get quotes for Chevrolet 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Chevrolet 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0-litre petrol engine powered the Chevrolet Spark city car throughout its first two generations (2005–2015) and represents Chevrolet’s smallest displacement sold in the UK. This size uses engine codes B10D1 and B10S1 — a four-cylinder, 16-valve unit that is simple and cheap to replace when it fails. The most common failure that drives replacement queries is cylinder head cracking between valve seats, which causes coolant loss, misfires, and combustion gas ingress into the cooling system. For a car worth £1,000–£2,000, a used or reconditioned 1.0-litre engine is almost always better value than a major cylinder head repair at a dealer
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Chevrolet 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2-litre petrol engine was Chevrolet’s most common small displacement in the UK, fitted to both the Spark and Aveo across most of their production runs (2005–2015). Engine codes B12D1 and B12DT (the latter a rare turbo version) power these city cars and superminis. The 1.2 shares the same fundamental design as the 1.0-litre and suffers from identical cylinder head cracking problems, particularly around the exhaust valve seats. Replacement demand is high for this size because the labour cost to properly diagnose and replace a head often exceeds the value of a used engine. A reconditioned 1.2-litre long engine with a fresh head and gasket set is the recommended repair path
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet 1.2L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: timing chain noise on high-mileage units

items.title: Chevrolet 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4-litre petrol appears in two very different forms across Chevrolet’s UK range. The naturally aspirated 1.4 (engine code B14D1) powered early Cruze models and some Aveo variants, while the 1.4 Turbo (engine codes B14NET, A14NET, LUJ) is a direct-injection turbocharged unit that powered higher-spec Cruze models (2009–2015). The turbocharged 1.4 is the one that generates replacement demand: it suffers from piston ring carbonisation due to direct injection, leading to high oil consumption, failed catalytic converters, and eventually cylinder scoring. Timing chain stretch on higher-mileage B14NET units is also common. Reconditioned 1.4 Turbo engines must include new piston rings and a decarbonised head
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Chevrolet 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6-litre petrol was fitted to the Chevrolet Cruze (2009–2015) and the earlier Chevrolet Lacetti (2004–2008), representing the larger naturally aspirated displacement in Chevrolet’s small-car range. Engine codes include B16D1 (Cruze) and F16D4 (Lacetti). These engines are generally robust compared to the smaller B-series petrol units, but they do suffer from camshaft position sensor failures that cause running issues and can, if ignored, lead to timing variation and valve contact. Coil pack failures and catalytic converter degradation are also common as mileage exceeds 80,000 miles. Replacement demand is moderate; a used 1.6-litre engine is affordable and widely available
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Chevrolet 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8-litre petrol was Chevrolet’s top naturally aspirated displacement in the UK, fitted to the Chevrolet Orlando MPV (2011–2015) and the Chevrolet Lacetti (2004–2008). Engine codes include F18D4 (Orlando) and F18D3 (Lacetti). This displacement is less common and therefore generates fewer replacement queries, but failures do occur — typically around the variable valve timing (VVT) system, which can stick and cause cam timing correlation codes and poor running. The timing belt (not chain) requires regular replacement at 60,000 miles; failure to do so is catastrophic. Low-mileage used engines are the most economical replacement route
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Chevrolet 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre petrol is a rare displacement in Chevrolet’s UK range, fitted only to a limited number of Chevrolet Cruze models (2011–2014) as the 2.0 petrol automatic, primarily for markets where diesel was less popular. Engine codes include LS2 variants (imported, not common). For UK Chevrolet owners, this size is exceptionally rare, and replacement queries are almost non-existent. If you own a 2.0-litre petrol Cruze, replacement engines are available but may require sourcing from European breakers. Standard 2.0 petrol failure points include coil pack failure and, on higher-mileage units, oil consumption from worn piston rings
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet 2.0L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Chevrolet needs? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine code and displacement — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Chevrolet specialists.

fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Chevrolet engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known brand‑wide issues, and UK specialist supply — from the most popular diesel workhorses to the small-capacity petrol city car units.

items.title: Chevrolet Diesel Engines
items.description: Chevrolet diesel engines cover many of the brand's highest-mileage UK models. Replacement demand is usually driven by timing system wear, injector and turbo faults, plus EGR or DPF-related damage once repeat short-journey use starts affecting reliability.
importantNotes: Short-journey use increases DPF, EGR, and oil-dilution risk on many diesel engines.
A documented service history matters more on diesel replacements because injector, turbo, and timing faults often build gradually.
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet diesel engine prices

items.title: Chevrolet Petrol Engines
items.description: Chevrolet petrol engines span everything from core daily-driver models to higher-output performance variants. Most replacement enquiries start with timing chain noise, oil consumption, cooling-system failure, or internal wear that makes a warranted replacement more sensible than repeated repairs.
importantNotes: Cold-start rattles, overheating history, and oil use are the biggest warning signs before a petrol engine replacement becomes necessary.
Direct-injection petrol engines often need specialist attention for carbon build-up alongside the main mechanical repair.
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.closing: Not sure which fuel type your Chevrolet has? Enter your registration number above to instantly identify your model, engine code, fuel system and compatible replacement options — all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Chevrolet specialists.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Chevrolet Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Chevrolet engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Chevrolet specialists.

entries.title: Z20S 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: The Z20S (also known as Z20DMH or LLW) is a 2.0-litre VM Motori turbo-diesel producing 150bhp. It was fitted to the Chevrolet Captiva (C100, 2006–2011) and Chevrolet Cruze (J300, 2009–2015). The most common failure driving replacement is single-row timing chain stretch, which causes rattling on start-up and eventual valve damage if ignored. Injector failure (black smoke, poor starting) is also common. A quality rebuilt unit includes an uprated timing chain kit, reconditioned injectors with new nozzles, and a revised oil pump.
entries.cta: Get quotes for Z20S Chevrolet engine replacement

entries.title: Z20S1 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: The Z20S1 (Z20DM, LMN) is a lower-output variant of the 2.0-litre VCDi family producing 121–127bhp. It appears in the Chevrolet Captiva (C100, early models) and Chevrolet Lacetti (J200, diesel variant, limited UK volume). This engine shares the timing chain weakness of the Z20S, with chain stretch typically occurring between 80,000–100,000 miles. Rebuilt units include an upgraded timing kit and revised tensioner.

entries.title: Z20D1 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: The Z20D1 (LNP) is a later 2.0-litre VCDi diesel producing 163bhp, fitted to the Chevrolet Cruze (J300, 2010–2015), Chevrolet Orlando (J309, 2010–2018), and Chevrolet Malibu (2012–2016). Timing chain stretch is the dominant failure, with plastic guide wear accelerated by extended oil change intervals. Injector failure is also common. A quality rebuilt unit includes an uprated timing chain and tensioner kit, reconditioned injectors, and a new oil pump.

entries.title: Z22D1 2.2L Diesel
entries.description: The Z22D1 (LNQ) is a 2.2-litre VCDi diesel producing 163–184bhp, fitted exclusively to the later Chevrolet Captiva (C140, 2010–2015). This engine shares the timing chain issues of the 2.0-litre family but also suffers from oil cooler gasket failure (causing coolant-oil emulsion) and injector failure. A quality rebuilt unit includes an upgraded timing kit, new oil cooler and gaskets, reconditioned injectors, and a revised crankcase breather system.
entries.cta: Get quotes for Z22D1 Chevrolet engine replacement

entries.title: B10D1 1.0L Petrol
entries.description: The B10D1 is a 1.0-litre 16-valve petrol engine producing 68bhp. It was fitted to the Chevrolet Spark (M300, 2009–2015). Coil pack failure (causing misfires, EML illumination, and catalytic converter damage if ignored) is the most common reason owners seek replacement. Thermostat housing leaks are also common. A quality rebuilt unit includes new OEM-spec ignition coils, upgraded spark plugs, and a replacement thermostat housing.

entries.title: B12D1 1.2L Petrol
entries.description: The B12D1 is a 1.2-litre 16-valve petrol engine producing 84bhp. It was fitted to the Chevrolet Spark (M300) and Chevrolet Aveo (T300, 2011–2015). Coil pack failure is the dominant issue, similar to the B10D1. Lower-volume failures include timing chain stretch on higher-mileage units. A quality rebuilt unit includes new ignition coils, a timing chain kit, and a full gasket set.
entries.cta: Get quotes for B12D1 Chevrolet engine replacement

entries.title: A12XER 1.2L Petrol
entries.description: The A12XER is a 1.2-litre Family 0 petrol engine (shared with Vauxhall) producing 86bhp. It was fitted to the Chevrolet Aveo (T250, 2008–2011) and Chevrolet Kalos (2005–2011). This engine suffers from two catastrophic failures: timing chain stretch (causing rattling and eventual valve damage) and piston ring carbonisation (causing oil consumption of 1L per 1,000 miles or more). A quality rebuilt unit includes an uprated timing chain kit and redesigned low-tension piston rings to address oil consumption.

entries.title: LE5 / LE9 / LEA 2.4L Petrol
entries.description: The LE5, LE9, and LEA are 2.4-litre Ecotec petrol engines producing 167–171bhp. They were fitted to the Chevrolet Captiva (C100 and C140) and Chevrolet Malibu (2012–2016). Timing chain stretch is the number one failure — cheap replacement chains often fail within 20,000 miles. Oil consumption from clogged piston rings is also common. A quality rebuilt unit includes a genuine or OEM-grade timing chain kit with metal guides (not plastic), redesigned piston rings, and a new oil pump.

entries.title: A30XH / LF1 3.0L Petrol
entries.description: The A30XH (also known as LF1) is a 3.0-litre Alloytec V6 producing 249–258bhp. It was fitted to the Chevrolet Captiva (C140, 2010–2015) as the 3.0 V6 petrol. Timing chain issues (stretched chains causing rattle on start-up) and camshaft actuator solenoid failure are the primary replacement drivers. A quality rebuilt unit includes a full timing chain kit (all three chains), new camshaft actuators, and revised chain guides.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 3.0L Chevrolet V6 engines

entries.title: 10HM 3.2L Petrol
entries.description: The 10HM is a 3.2-litre Alloytec V6 producing 228bhp. It was fitted to the Chevrolet Captiva (C100, 2006–2011) and the related Opel Antara. This engine has a reputation for timing chain failure — particularly the left bank — and water pump seal leakage. A quality rebuilt unit includes a full timing chain kit (all three chains) with upgraded tensioners, a new water pump, and revised chain guides.

families.name: LS V8 Performance Family (1997–present – Import Market)

entries.title: LS3 6.2L Petrol
entries.description: The LS3 is a 6.2-litre pushrod V8 producing 426–436bhp. It was fitted to the Chevrolet Camaro SS (2010–2015) and Chevrolet Corvette C6 (2008–2013) — both imported vehicles in the UK. The most common failures driving replacement are collapsed hydraulic roller lifters (causing camshaft lobe wear) and oil pump pressure relief valve failure. A quality rebuilt unit includes upgraded lifters (Johnson or OEM+), a high-volume oil pump, new cam bearings, and a performance head gasket set.
entries.cta: Get quotes for LS3 Chevrolet engine replacement

entries.title: LS1 5.7L Petrol
entries.description: The LS1 is a 5.7-litre pushrod V8 producing 305–350bhp. It was fitted to the Chevrolet Corvette C5 (1997–2004) and imported Chevrolet Camaro (1998–2002). Piston slap on cold start is common (generally harmless), but rear main seal oil leaks and lifter tick are the primary failure points that lead owners to consider replacement. A quality rebuilt unit includes new lifters, a rear main seal with upgraded PTFE, and a high-volume oil pump.

entries.title: LS2 6.0L Petrol
entries.description: The LS2 is a 6.0-litre pushrod V8 producing 395–400bhp. It was fitted to the Chevrolet Corvette C6 (2005–2007) and imported Chevrolet SSR. Lifter tick and oil pump wear are the dominant failures. A quality rebuilt unit includes a high-volume oil pump, upgraded lifters, and a full gasket set.

entries.title: LQ4 / LQ9 6.0L Petrol
entries.description: The LQ4 and LQ9 are 6.0-litre Vortec V8s producing 300–345bhp. They appear in imported Chevrolet Silverado 1500, Suburban, and Tahoe vehicles. Active Fuel Management (AFM) lifter collapse on LS4/LMG variants is the most catastrophic failure, wiping the camshaft lobe and requiring full top-end rebuild. A quality rebuilt unit includes an AFM-delete kit (standard lifters and valley cover), a new camshaft, and a high-volume oil pump.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6.0L Chevrolet Vortec engines

engineCodeDirectory.closing: Can't find your engine code listed above? Use our full Chevrolet engine code directory below — every code from the last 35 years — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.

directory.h3: Full Chevrolet Engine Code Directory (All Years)
directory.label: View all Chevrolet engine codes (70+ codes, 1990–2025)
directory.intro: A comprehensive alphabetical listing of every Chevrolet engine code from the last 35 years. Use the search above or enter your registration for an instant match to your specific engine. All codes link to a quote request.

engineYears.h2: Chevrolet Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section maps every major engine change across the Chevrolet model range - from the Gen III LS V8 era of the late 1990s through Ecotec four-cylinders, Duramax diesels, EcoTec3 truck engines, and the Gen V LT performance family - so owners can identify the exact engine fitted to their Chevrolet by year and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK and international specialists.

years.preview: Gen III LS Era: Small-Block Revolution Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: 1997 marked one of the most significant moments in Chevrolet engine history - the debut of the all-aluminium Gen III LS small-block V8 in the C5 Corvette, replacing the LT1 and establishing the LS architecture that would underpin every Chevrolet performance vehicle for the next two decades.
keyChanges: LS1 5.7-litre Gen III small-block introduced in the C5 Corvette - all-aluminium construction, coil-near-plug ignition, and cathedral-port cylinder heads replace the previous LT1 architecture
Vortec 4.8 (LR4) and 5.3 (LM7) Gen III truck variants introduced from 1999 in Silverado, Tahoe and Suburban - bringing LS architecture to the full-size truck range
LS6 high-performance variant of the 5.7-litre introduced in the C5 Z06 from 2001, producing 385 bhp in stock form
6.0-litre LQ4 iron-block Gen III introduced for heavy-duty truck applications from 1999
5.7-litre Vortec Gen I phased out across truck range as Gen III Vortec units roll in
mainEngines: LS1 - 5.7-litre V8 Gen III (Corvette C5, Camaro 4th Gen, Pontiac Firebird)
LS6 - 5.7-litre V8 high-performance (Corvette C5 Z06)
LR4 Vortec 4800 - 4.8-litre V8 Gen III (Silverado, Tahoe, Suburban)
LM7 Vortec 5300 - 5.3-litre V8 Gen III iron-block (Silverado, Tahoe, GMC Sierra)
LQ4 - 6.0-litre V8 Gen III iron-block (Silverado 2500HD, Suburban 2500)
knownFor: LS1 is widely regarded as one of the greatest production V8 architectures ever made - robust, high-revving, and exceptionally tunable
Gen III LS iron and aluminium blocks both respond well to high mileage; AFM/DOD not yet fitted so no lifter collapse risk on early units
LM7 5.3-litre truck engine is a proven workhorse with documented 250,000-mile examples under regular service schedules
Early LS1 units can develop intake manifold coolant leaks from the composite lower manifold - a well-known and affordable fix with aftermarket solutions
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Chevrolet Corvette C5 LS1 engine replacement - Chevrolet Silverado 5.3 Vortec engine for sale - LS1 rebuilt engine UK - Chevrolet Camaro 5.7 engine replacement
years.cta: Search for 1997–2004 Chevrolet LS and Vortec engine replacements - compare UK and European specialist prices

years.preview: Ecotec Family Launch: Four-Cylinder Global Engine Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The GM Ecotec family - Chevrolet's first truly modern four-cylinder architecture - arrived in 2000 and rapidly became the standard engine across all Chevrolet passenger car lines globally, including the Cavalier, Astra-based models and early Cobalt.
keyChanges: Ecotec 2.2-litre (L61) introduced from 2000 - all-aluminium DOHC four-cylinder replacing the ageing OHV Iron Duke and 2.4 Twin Cam in passenger cars
2.0-litre Ecotec turbo (LNF) developed for Cobalt SS from 2005, producing 260 bhp - first turbocharged Chevrolet four-cylinder performance application
1.8-litre Ecotec variants (Z18XE / Z18XER) fitted to European market models including Chevrolet-badged Opel/Vauxhall derivatives
Family I 1.4-litre and 1.6-litre units continue in Aveo/Kalos for entry-level global market
Variable valve timing (VVT) introduced on later Ecotec 2.2 variants from 2006
mainEngines: L61 Ecotec 2.2 - 2.2-litre DOHC four-cylinder petrol (Cavalier, Cobalt, Malibu)
LNF Ecotec 2.0T - 2.0-litre turbocharged four-cylinder (Cobalt SS, HHR SS)
Z18XE / Z18XER - 1.8-litre Ecotec (European Chevrolet Lacetti, Astra-based derivatives)
Family I 1.4 / 1.6 - (Chevrolet Aveo / Kalos)
knownFor: Ecotec 2.2 L61 is a reliable and broadly supported engine but timing chain tensioner wear is documented from around 100,000 miles
LNF 2.0T is a high-output unit for its era; intercooler and bypass valve failures are the most commonly reported specialist concerns
European Ecotec derivatives benefit from strong Vauxhall/Opel parts interchangeability - making UK parts supply very accessible
Family I 1.4 and 1.6 units in the Aveo are simple and durable but offer limited performance headroom and are sensitive to oil level neglect
years.cta: Find replacement Ecotec engines for your 2000–2007 Chevrolet - compare rebuilt four-cylinder options from UK specialists

years.preview: Gen IV LS & Vortec: AFM, VVT and 6.2-Litre Expansion Major Reliability Revision
years.description: The Gen IV LS family launched in 2005 brought Active Fuel Management (AFM/DOD), variable valve timing, and the expansion to 6.2-litre displacements - alongside the introduction of the new LS2, LS3, LS7 and LSA performance engines that would define Chevrolet's performance identity for a decade.
keyChanges: LS2 6.0-litre Gen IV introduced in the C6 Corvette (2005) and Pontiac GTO - successor to LS6, featuring improved rectangular-port heads
Active Fuel Management (AFM) - cylinder deactivation - introduced across Gen IV 5.3 truck engines from 2007, designed to improve fuel economy
LS3 6.2-litre introduced in C6 Corvette from 2008, producing 430 bhp standard - becomes the new naturally aspirated benchmark
LS7 7.0-litre dry-sump naturally aspirated V8 introduced in C6 Z06 from 2006, producing 505 bhp - the most powerful naturally aspirated small-block ever fitted to a production Chevrolet
LSA 6.2-litre supercharged introduced in 5th Gen Camaro ZL1 (2012) and Cadillac CTS-V; LS9 supercharged fitted to C6 ZR1 from 2009
58x reluctor wheel replaces 24x across Gen IV range - a key identification point for engine swaps and replacements
mainEngines: LS2 - 6.0-litre V8 Gen IV (Corvette C6, Pontiac GTO, Chevrolet SSR)
LS3 - 6.2-litre V8 Gen IV (Corvette C6, Camaro 5th Gen, Camaro Grand Sport)
LS7 - 7.0-litre V8 naturally aspirated (Corvette C6 Z06, Camaro Z/28)
LSA - 6.2-litre supercharged V8 (Camaro ZL1 5th Gen)
LS9 - 6.2-litre supercharged V8 (Corvette C6 ZR1)
LY5 / LMG / LC9 - 5.3-litre Gen IV V8 (Silverado, Tahoe, Suburban, Colorado)
L92 / L9H - 6.2-litre Gen IV V8 (Escalade, Tahoe, Sierra Denali)
popularModels: Chevrolet Corvette C6 (including Z06 and ZR1)
knownFor: AFM/DOD cylinder deactivation on Gen IV 5.3 truck engines is the dominant failure concern - collapsed lifters and AFM lifter tray failures are widely reported from 80,000–120,000 miles, particularly on LMG, LC9 and LY5 units
LS3 and LS7 are regarded as near-bulletproof in naturally aspirated form; LS7 titanium intake valve issues on track-use examples are documented
LSA supercharger drive belt and intercooler heat exchanger are service items on high-mileage Camaro ZL1 examples
Gen IV 58x reluctor wheel change from Gen III 24x means mixed-generation engine swaps require reluctor wheel exchange - a critical identification point for replacement buyers
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Chevrolet Silverado 5.3 AFM lifter failure engine replacement - Chevrolet Camaro 6.2 LS3 engine for sale - Corvette C6 LS2 engine replacement UK - Chevrolet 5.3 DOD delete engine - Camaro ZL1 LSA engine replacement
years.cta: Compare 2005–2013 Chevrolet LS and Vortec V8 replacement engines - AFM-deleted and standard options from UK specialists

years.preview: Ecotec Turbocharged 1.4 & Cruze Era: Global Four-Cylinder Reinvention Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The second-generation Ecotec turbocharged 1.4-litre (LUJ/LUV) became the defining engine of the Chevrolet Cruze era - a compact, efficient, and globally deployed unit that also underpinned the Sonic and Trax in the European and UK market.
keyChanges: Ecotec 1.4T (LUJ / LUV) turbocharged four-cylinder introduced in Chevrolet Cruze from 2011 - replacing the naturally aspirated 1.6 and 1.8 as the core global Chevrolet compact engine
2.0-litre Ecotec diesel (LNP) introduced in Cruze for European and UK markets from 2009 - the primary diesel option for UK Cruze buyers
1.6-litre and 1.8-litre naturally aspirated Ecotecs continue in Sonic / Aveo for entry-level positions
2.5-litre Ecotec naturally aspirated (LKW) introduced in Malibu from 2012 as a mid-size four-cylinder step above the 1.4T
Euro 5 emissions compliance across European Chevrolet lineup by 2011
mainEngines: LUJ / LUV Ecotec 1.4T - 1.4-litre turbocharged four-cylinder petrol (Chevrolet Cruze, Chevrolet Sonic, Chevrolet Trax)
LNP Ecotec 2.0 diesel - 2.0-litre turbocharged diesel (Chevrolet Cruze diesel, UK market)
LKW Ecotec 2.5 - 2.5-litre naturally aspirated four-cylinder (Chevrolet Malibu 7th Gen)
Z16XER / Z18XER - 1.6 / 1.8-litre Ecotec (Chevrolet Aveo, Captiva entry)
2.4 Ecotec (LE5 / LAF) - 2.4-litre four-cylinder (Chevrolet Malibu, Captiva)
knownFor: Ecotec 1.4T (LUJ) is a broadly capable engine but cracked pistons are the most widely reported serious failure mode - typically manifesting at 30,000–60,000 miles in 2011–2014 Cruze models
Coolant leaks from the turbo coolant feed line and water pump are persistent complaints on 2011–2013 Cruze 1.4T, sometimes leading to overheating and secondary engine damage
Cruze 2.0 diesel benefits from the reliability of the proven Fiat/GM diesel architecture but EGR fouling and DPF clogging are reported from 70,000 miles in UK stop-start use
Captiva 2.4 is a dependable naturally aspirated unit with good parts availability; timing chain service at higher mileage is the primary recommended preventative measure
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Chevrolet Cruze 1.4 turbo engine replacement - Chevrolet Cruze engine failure cracked piston - Chevrolet Cruze 2.0 diesel engine for sale UK - Chevrolet Sonic 1.4T engine replacement - Cruze LUJ engine rebuild UK
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008–2016 Chevrolet Cruze, Sonic and Trax replacement engines - compare rebuilt 1.4T and 2.0 diesel options

years.preview: Gen V LT Small-Block: Direct Injection & EcoTec3 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The Gen V LT family launched in 2014 represented the biggest architectural change to the Chevrolet small-block since 1997 - switching to direct fuel injection, variable valve timing, oil-spray piston cooling, and an all-new combustion chamber design across every performance and truck application.
keyChanges: LT1 6.2-litre Gen V introduced in the C7 Corvette (2014) - direct injection, dry-sump lubrication, and variable valve timing produce 460 bhp from a naturally aspirated V8
LT4 6.2-litre supercharged Gen V introduced in Corvette Z06 and Camaro ZL1 6th Gen from 2015–2017, producing 650 bhp - the most powerful production Chevrolet engine to date at launch
EcoTec3 L83 5.3-litre and L86 6.2-litre Gen V truck engines replace Gen IV units from 2014 in Silverado and Tahoe - bringing direct injection to full-size truck line
AFM (Active Fuel Management) retained in L83 5.3 EcoTec3 - DOD lifter failure concern carries forward from Gen IV units
LT5 6.2-litre supercharged introduced in C7 Corvette ZR1 from 2019, producing 755 bhp with a 2.65-litre Eaton supercharger - the most powerful small-block Chevrolet ever built
mainEngines: LT1 - 6.2-litre Gen V V8 naturally aspirated DI (Corvette C7, Camaro 6th Gen SS)
LT4 - 6.2-litre Gen V supercharged V8 (Corvette C7 Z06, Camaro ZL1 6th Gen)
LT5 - 6.2-litre Gen V supercharged V8 755 bhp (Corvette C7 ZR1)
L83 EcoTec3 5.3 - 5.3-litre V8 DI (Silverado 1500, Tahoe, Suburban, Colorado)
L86 EcoTec3 6.2 - 6.2-litre V8 DI (Silverado LTZ/High Country, Tahoe, Yukon Denali)
L8T - 6.6-litre V8 Gen V (Silverado 2500HD/3500HD from 2020)
knownFor: LT1 Gen V is a refined and high-output naturally aspirated unit - widely praised for its balance of fuel economy and performance; direct injection brings the common carbon fouling on intake valves at higher mileage
L83 EcoTec3 5.3 AFM lifter failure continues to be the primary truck engine concern - DOD delete kits and AFM-disabled replacement engines are in high demand across the Silverado/Tahoe community
LT4 supercharger intercooler water pump is the critical service item; heat soak can be an issue in extended track use
LT5 at 755 bhp remains one of the benchmark normally road-legal V8 performance engines globally - specialist rebuild demand is growing as early C7 ZR1 examples accumulate mileage
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Chevrolet Silverado 5.3 EcoTec3 AFM lifter failure - Chevrolet Corvette C7 LT1 engine replacement - Camaro 6.2 LT1 engine for sale - Silverado 5.3 DOD delete engine rebuild - Chevrolet Camaro ZL1 LT4 engine replacement
years.cta: Compare 2014–2019 Chevrolet Gen V LT and EcoTec3 engine replacements - AFM-deleted Silverado and performance Corvette/Camaro options from specialists

years.preview: Duramax Diesel Era: 2.8 Colorado & 3.0 Silverado Technology Introduction
years.description: The introduction of the Duramax 2.8-litre four-cylinder diesel to the Colorado (2015) and the later 3.0-litre inline-six Duramax to the Silverado 1500 (2019) marked Chevrolet's most ambitious diesel passenger/truck programme in decades - targeting both efficiency and capability in a diesel-averse North American market.
keyChanges: Duramax 2.8-litre four-cylinder turbodiesel (LWN) introduced in Chevrolet Colorado from 2015 MY - the first diesel in the mid-size Chevrolet pickup since the 1980s
3.0-litre Duramax inline-six turbodiesel introduced in Silverado 1500 from 2019 - the most efficient engine ever offered in a full-size Chevrolet pickup
Euro 6 compliance across all diesel units in European-market Chevrolet applications
DPF and AdBlue SCR systems standard on all new diesel variants
mainEngines: LWN Duramax 2.8 - 2.8-litre four-cylinder diesel (Chevrolet Colorado, GMC Canyon)
3.0 Duramax I6 (LM2) - 3.0-litre inline-six diesel (Chevrolet Silverado 1500 from 2019)
knownFor: Duramax 2.8 LWN is regarded as a durable and efficient unit for a mid-size pickup; high-mileage examples show strong longevity under correct service schedules
3.0 Duramax I6 in the Silverado delivers best-in-class diesel fuel economy for a full-size truck; B20 biodiesel compatibility is a notable UK-market advantage
DPF regeneration in stop-start driving cycles is the primary concern for UK owners - short-trip use without regular motorway runs leads to accelerated DPF failure
Both diesel units benefit from very strong Duramax specialist support network in the UK and Europe
years.cta: Get specialist quotes for Chevrolet Colorado 2.8 Duramax and Silverado 3.0 Duramax engine replacements

years.preview: C8 Corvette & Mid-Engine LT2/LT6: A New Era Performance Landmark
years.description: The 2020 mid-engine C8 Corvette introduced the LT2 - a comprehensively redesigned naturally aspirated 6.2-litre V8 in a dry-sump mid-engine configuration - fundamentally repositioning Chevrolet's performance engine programme and delivering its highest-revving naturally aspirated V8 with the LT6 in 2023.
keyChanges: LT2 6.2-litre Gen V naturally aspirated introduced in C8 Corvette Stingray (2020) - dry-sump, mid-mounted, 495 bhp; first mid-engine Corvette V8 in history
Z51 Performance Package LT2 raises output to 495 bhp with enhanced cooling and exhaust
LT6 5.5-litre flat-plane crank V8 introduced in C8 Corvette Z06 (2023) - 670 bhp naturally aspirated; the highest output naturally aspirated Chevrolet road car engine ever produced
E-Ray hybrid C8 variant introduced 2023 - LT2 V8 paired with electric front axle motor for all-wheel drive without a conventional gearbox
Silverado and Tahoe continue with L83/L86 EcoTec3 family with incremental DI refinements
mainEngines: LT2 - 6.2-litre Gen V naturally aspirated V8 mid-engine (Corvette C8 Stingray)
LT6 - 5.5-litre flat-plane crank naturally aspirated V8 (Corvette C8 Z06, producing 670 bhp)
LT2 + Electric Motor - hybrid system (Corvette C8 E-Ray)
L83 / L86 EcoTec3 (continuing) - 5.3 / 6.2-litre V8 (Silverado, Tahoe, Suburban)
knownFor: LT2 in the C8 is a precision-engineered unit; dry-sump oil system and mid-engine cooling demands require specialist attention at service - not a conventional garage job
LT6 flat-plane crank delivers a visceral high-revving character more akin to a racing engine than a production V8; early examples are accumulating mileage with broadly positive specialist sentiment
C8 E-Ray hybrid system adds battery and electric motor complexity; high-voltage servicing qualification required for UK independent specialists
Silverado AFM lifter failure issue remains unchanged in continuing EcoTec3 L83 units - buyers of 2019–2023 Silverado should verify AFM status before purchase
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Chevrolet Corvette C8 LT2 engine replacement - Corvette Z06 LT6 engine for sale - Silverado 5.3 AFM engine replacement 2020 - Chevrolet C8 engine specialist UK
years.cta: Source precision-rebuilt replacement engines for your 2019–2023 Chevrolet Corvette C8 or Silverado - compare UK specialist quotes now

years.preview: Silverado EV, Equinox EV & Blazer EV: Electrification Transition Technology Introduction
years.description: From 2022 onward, Chevrolet's powertrain direction shifted decisively toward the Ultium electric platform - delivering the Silverado EV, Equinox EV and Blazer EV while the internal combustion engine range continues in parallel for Silverado HD, Corvette, and Camaro successors.
keyChanges: Ultium platform electric motors introduced across Chevrolet EV range - Silverado EV (2024), Equinox EV (2024), Blazer EV (2023)
Silverado EV produces up to 664 bhp from dual Ultium electric motors - eclipsing every ICE Silverado engine output figure
ICE Silverado HD (2500/3500) continues with 6.6-litre Duramax V8 diesel (L5P) and 6.6-litre petrol V8 L8T alongside Ultium EV siblings
Gen VI small-block V8 confirmed in development (2023 announcement) for future full-size truck and SUV applications
Camaro 6th Gen production ends 2024; no direct ICE successor confirmed as of 2026
mainEngines: Ultium Dual Motor - electric (Silverado EV, Equinox EV, Blazer EV)
L5P Duramax 6.6 V8 diesel - (Silverado HD 2500/3500 continuing)
L8T 6.6 V8 petrol - (Silverado HD 2500/3500 continuing)
LT1 / LT4 - (Camaro final production run to 2024)
knownFor: Ultium platform delivers class-leading EV range and towing capacity; as a new architecture, long-term replacement demand is emerging and UK specialist tooling investment is underway
L5P Duramax 6.6 diesel is the most capable and refined Duramax ever produced - widely regarded as a heritage-grade engine for demanding commercial and towing applications
Camaro LT1 and LT4 final production examples are already attracting collector attention - early sourcing of replacement engines for known-good examples is an active market
Gen VI small-block confirmation signals Chevrolet's commitment to maintaining V8 ICE alongside electrification - a unique dual-pathway strategy among major global OEMs
years.cta: Get ahead of replacement demand - source Chevrolet Silverado, Camaro and Corvette engines from UK-based specialists before stock tightens

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Chevrolet was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Chevrolet specialists.

faq.h2: Chevrolet Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: ** Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Chevrolet owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Chevrolet engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Expect to pay between £1,600 and £4,200 for a reconditioned Chevrolet engine supplied and fitted by a UK specialist, depending on the model and engine size. A used Chevrolet engine for a Spark or Aveo starts around £1,600–£2,200 fitted, while a 2.0 or 2.2 VCDi diesel for a Captiva or Cruze will typically cost £2,800–£4,200 with a warranty and upgraded components. Fitting alone (supply‑only) across most Chevrolet engines is usually £600–£1,000 at an independent garage. The best way to get an accurate price is to compare quotes for your specific engine code.
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet engine replacement prices now

items.question: What affects the cost of a Chevrolet engine replacement?
items.answer: Four main factors determine the final price: engine size and rarity (a 2.2 VCDi Z22D1 costs more than a common 1.2 B12D1), engine condition (reconditioned with upgraded parts costs more than a used pull‑out), vehicle age and complexity, and whether you choose supply‑only or supply & fit. Labour rates also vary by location — a specialist in the South East may charge 20–30% more than one in the North. Our comparison tool gives you transparent pricing across multiple UK suppliers so you can see exactly what affects your quote.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Chevrolet engine specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Chevrolet?
items.answer: For most Chevrolet models — particularly the Captiva, Cruze, and Spark — replacing the engine is financially sensible if the chassis, gearbox, and bodywork are in good condition. A reconditioned VCDi diesel with upgraded timing chain components and a new fuel system will often outlast the original engine, giving you another 80,000+ miles for £2,800–£4,200. The alternative — scrapping a serviceable car and buying a replacement — will typically cost you £3,000–£6,000 for a comparable used Chevrolet. If your vehicle is otherwise sound, replacement is almost always worth it.
items.cta: Use our free comparison tool to see whether a rebuilt or used Chevrolet engine makes financial sense

items.question: What's included in a Chevrolet engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: A full supply & fit package includes sourcing the correct engine for your Chevrolet model and code, removing the old unit, installing the replacement (used, reconditioned, or rebuilt), and replacing all consumables: engine oil, coolant, filters, and often a new cambelt or timing chain kit. The service typically includes a workmanship warranty (usually 12 months or 12,000 miles), proper disposal of your old engine, and a post‑installation test‑drive to confirm everything runs correctly. Fitting costs across Chevrolet engines generally add £600–£1,000 to the supply price.
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet engine supply and fit deals

items.question: How long does a Chevrolet engine replacement take?
items.answer: For a common Chevrolet model like a Spark 1.2 or Cruze 1.6 petrol, a straightforward engine swap typically takes 3–5 working days from the time the supplier has the replacement unit on hand. More complex models — particularly the Captiva 2.0 and 2.2 VCDi diesels — may take 5–7 working days due to the additional labour around the turbo, DPF, and fuel system. The biggest variable is engine availability; our network of UK Chevrolet specialists stocks commonly replaced engine codes like Z20D1, Z22D1, and B12D1, which speeds up lead times considerably.
items.cta: Find rebuilt Chevrolet engines with fast turnaround times

items.question: Do reconditioned Chevrolet engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes. Every reconditioned Chevrolet engine supplied through our marketplace comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty as standard. Many suppliers also offer extended 24‑month coverage on rebuilt and remanufactured units, particularly for the VCDi diesel range where timing chain and fuel pump upgrades are included. The warranty typically covers major internal components: crankshaft, pistons, conrods, timing chain, oil pump, and cylinder head, with the supplier covering both parts and labour for any failure within the warranty period. Always confirm exact coverage and any exclusions directly with your chosen specialist before proceeding.
items.cta: Compare reconditioned Chevrolet engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Chevrolet engine?
items.answer: A used Chevrolet engine is the cheaper upfront option — typically £1,600–£2,800 supplied & fitted — and makes sense if you are selling the car soon or working with a very tight budget. However, a reconditioned engine (£2,800–£4,200) includes new piston rings, bearings, gaskets, upgraded timing components (critical for VCDi diesels), and a 12–24 month warranty. For Chevrolet engines with known failure patterns — particularly the 2.0 and 2.2 VCDi timing chain, the 1.0/1.2 B‑series cylinder head, and the 1.4 Turbo direct‑injection carbon issues — a reconditioned unit with upgraded parts is strongly recommended for long‑term ownership.
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned Chevrolet engine prices side by side

items.question: Where can I find Chevrolet engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK‑wide network of independent Chevrolet engine specialists and reconditioners who carry stock for all common models — Spark, Aveo, Cruze, Captiva, and Orlando. Simply enter your registration number or engine code, and our platform matches you with the nearest suppliers who have your specific engine in stock or can source it quickly. While there are no exclusive Chevrolet specialist hubs, our network covers every region including the South East, Midlands, North West, Scotland, and Wales. Nationwide delivery is also available if you prefer to supply your own garage for fitting.
items.cta: Find Chevrolet engine specialists in your area now

items.question: What are the most common Chevrolet engine failures?
items.answer: The most common catastrophic failures driving Chevrolet engine replacement in the UK are: timing chain stretch on 2.0 and 2.2 VCDi diesels (Z20S, Z20D1, Z22D1) — chain jumps, bends valves; high‑pressure fuel pump debris contamination across the same VCDi family — destroys injectors and fuel rail; cylinder head cracking on 1.0 (B10D1) and 1.2 (B12D1) petrol engines in Spark and Aveo — coolant loss and compression failure; piston ring carbonisation on 1.4 Turbo (B14NET, A14NET) in Cruze — oil consumption and catalytic converter failure; and crankshaft bearing seizure on early 2.0 VCDi units — rapid failure with little warning. Most of these failures lead directly to replacement because repair costs quickly exceed the value of a reconditioned engine.
items.cta: Get quotes for replacement engines for common Chevrolet failures

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Chevrolet engine?
items.answer: The single most effective way to get the best price is to compare multiple quotes — prices for the same engine code can vary by £1,000 or more between suppliers. If you have a tight budget, a used engine with a short warranty is the cheapest way back on the road. For long‑term ownership, invest in a reconditioned unit with upgraded components; it costs more upfront but will last 80,000+ miles. Always ask suppliers to break down exactly what is included: whether fitting, delivery, VAT, and the old unit disposal are covered. Finally, consider supply‑only if you already have a trusted garage that will fit the engine for less than our network rate.
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet engine deals from multiple UK suppliers
